title: Infrastructure-as-Code Deployment
when_to_use: Use Infrastructure-as-Code Deployment to develop professional-grade infrastructure-as-code deployment capabilities at Mastery level. This recipe is part of the Adversary Infrastructure at Scale pillar and integrates with linked missions OM-M06, OM-M13.

steps.title: Multi-Region Architecture Design
steps.instruction: Design resilient infrastructure spanning 3+ African cloud regions or international providers. Architecture includes: team server (C2 backend), HTTP/HTTPS redirectors, DNS redirectors, monitoring/alerting, engagement isolation. Document isolation model: separate VPC per engagement, credential isolation, log separation. Include network diagrams, firewall rules, data flow.
steps.tip: Multi-region architecture prevents single point of failure. Engagement isolation prevents compromise from cascading.
steps.expected_output: Architecture design (8+ pages) with network diagrams

steps.title: Terraform Infrastructure Code
steps.instruction: Write production-grade Terraform modules for: VPC creation, EC2/droplet instances, security groups, load balancers, database setup. Modules: team_server (internal), http_redirector (external), dns_redirector (external), monitoring_stack. Include variables for multi-region parameterization. Code must be version-controlled and reviewed.
steps.tip: Terraform code should be idempotent and reviewed like production code. Use separate terraform workspaces per engagement.
steps.expected_output: Complete Terraform codebase for multi-region deployment

steps.title: Domain & Certificate Automation
steps.instruction: Automate domain registration (via API), DNS configuration, and TLS certificate provisioning. Script: register random domain, configure DNS records (A, CNAME, MX), provision Let's Encrypt certificate, deploy certificate to redirectors. Automation must support rapid domain rotation on burn. Measure: time from domain registration to live redirector.
steps.tip: Domain automation reduces operator burden and enables rapid pivoting. Use DNS API integrations for automation.

steps.title: Monitoring & Alerting
steps.instruction: Implement health monitoring: team server availability, redirector response times, certificate expiration tracking, traffic anomalies. Alerting for: team server downtime, failed redirector health checks, unusual traffic patterns (potential detected redirector), certificate expiration. Use Prometheus/Grafana or CloudWatch for visualization.
steps.tip: Monitoring enables burn detection: sustained unusual traffic patterns suggest discovered infrastructure.

steps.title: Burn & Rebuild Procedure
steps.instruction: Test complete burn-and-rebuild procedure: destroy all infrastructure, rebuild fresh with new domains and certificates, verify C2 connectivity. Measure time from "burn decision" to "new infrastructure live". Target: <30 minutes for complete rebuild. Document: runbook, prerequisite checklist, verification steps.
steps.tip: Burn procedures must be tested before engagements. Timing matters when detection is imminent.
steps.expected_output: Burn procedure documentation + timed rebuild demo

steps.title: Cost Analysis & Provider Comparison
steps.instruction: Compare African and international cloud providers: pricing, data residency, VPN/proxy requirements, billing transparency. Build cost model: team server (always-on), redirectors (variable), data transfer. Optimize: spot instances, reserved capacity, cross-region transfer costs. Document: pricing breakdown, provider comparison, cost-saving strategies.
steps.tip: Infrastructure costs add up quickly. Optimize for engagement duration: 2-week engagement vs 6-month engagement have different optimal architectures.
steps.expected_output: Cost analysis with provider comparison matrix
